outlook_paragraph: Continental Resources (CTRA) shows below-average drilling activity with only 13 active sites at decision date, 1.2 standard deviations below the trailing norm, and minimal new pad activation. This suggests constrained near-term production growth into Q4 2022. With WTI averaging $64.42 and realized prices at a discount (78% of benchmark), revenue is further pressured. The company's 40% segment fraction indicates partial exposure to the monitored activity, limiting visibility into full operations. The deterministic revenue forecast is set at $2.12B.
